Hide delete button on dashboard when all players have voted (≥4 players) (#36)

Prevents accidental deletion of complete games with meaningful
participation by hiding the "🗑️ Delete game" button when every voter has
rated all songs and there are at least 4 voters.

- **`dashboard.tsx`**: Fetches voters and votes, computes
`hideDeleteButton` via a vote-count Map (O(n+m)), passes it to
`GameControls` in both open and closed game paths
- **`game-controls.tsx`**: Accepts optional `hideDeleteButton` prop,
conditionally renders the delete button

```tsx
const voteCountByVoter = new Map<string, number>();
for (const v of votes) {
  voteCountByVoter.set(v.voter, (voteCountByVoter.get(v.voter) ?? 0) + 1);
}
const hideDeleteButton =
  voters.length >= 4 &&
  totalSongs > 0 &&
  voters.every(
    (voter) => (voteCountByVoter.get(voter.name) ?? 0) >= totalSongs,
  );
```

---------

commit.message:
fix: track cumulative offset in memory across days (race condition)

Critical fix: async_add_external_statistics does NOT immediately commit
to the recorder DB. When processing multiple days in one update cycle,
reading the offset from DB for each day returned stale values, causing
all days to have overlapping cumulative sums.

Now follows enelgrid's pattern exactly:
1. Read offset ONCE from DB before the loop
2. Pass offset as parameter to _import_15min_statistics
3. Return updated offset for chaining to next day
4. Log at INFO level for easier debugging

Also changed _import_15min_statistics signature to accept and return
the cumulative offset instead of reading from DB internally.

Adds a development meta-skill at .claude/skills/quality-gate/ that performs
a complete quality gate analysis of the agents-initializer project.

The skill orchestrates three specialized subagents via the Task tool:
- artifact-inspector: static compliance checks on all 8 SKILL.md files,
  50+ reference files, 3 agent files, and template directories

  across all copies in both plugin and standalone distributions

  against the current skill instructions and references

If any checks fail, the skill generates a structured findings report at
.specs/reports/quality-gate-[date]-findings.md, pre-formatted for
/prp-core:prp-prd input to create a remediation PRD.

rpc: fix CValidationState incomplete type in csapp RPCs

CommitTransaction's CValidationState* parameter is optional (defaults to
nullptr). Remove the explicit CValidationState local variable — its full
definition lives in consensus/validation.h which is not pulled in by the
rpc/csapp.cpp include chain, causing 'incomplete type' build errors on CI.

commit.message:
fix: align statistics import with enelgrid reference pattern

Critical fixes based on enelgrid comparison:

1. Fix source/statistic_id mismatch: Changed statistic_id from
   'sensor:evn_smartmeter_consumption_15min' to 'evn_smartmeter:consumption_15min'
   so source field matches the statistic_id prefix (was: source=evn_smartmeter
   but prefix was 'sensor:')

2. Add recorder dependency in manifest.json: Ensures the recorder
   component loads BEFORE our integration, so async_add_external_statistics
   is available when we call it

3. Fix cumulative offset tracking: Was resetting to 0 each day, now
   reads last sum from DB via get_last_statistics so the sum field is
   monotonically increasing across days (required for has_sum=True)

4. Remove unused StatisticMeanType import (potential ImportError)

5. Remove unnecessary 15min sensor entity: External statistics don't
   need a companion sensor entity. The entity had TOTAL_INCREASING
   state_class which could conflict with external statistics.

6. Reduce scan interval from 30min to 4 hours (portal updates once/day)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat(gb): implement hardware-accurate DMG APU (all 4 channels) (#2028)

* feat(gb): implement hardware-accurate DMG APU (all 4 channels) (#2027)

Add a complete Game Boy DMG Audio Processing Unit under src/gb/apu/:

- CH1 (pulse + frequency sweep): duty cycle (4 patterns), length counter,
  volume envelope, sweep (period/direction/shift with overflow disable)
- CH2 (pulse, no sweep): duty cycle, length counter, volume envelope
- CH3 (wave output): 32-nibble wave RAM ($FF30–$FF3F), 4 output levels
  (mute/100%/50%/25%), length counter, half-rate frequency timer
- CH4 (noise): 15-bit/7-bit LFSR, 8-code divisor table, length counter,
  volume envelope
- Frame sequencer: 8-step/512 Hz; clocks length (steps 0/2/4/6),
  sweep (steps 2/6), envelope (step 7)
- Mixer: NR51 per-channel L/R panning, NR50 master volume, mono f32 output
- NR52 power-on/off: clears NR10–NR51 on power-off (DMG quirk: length
  counters remain writable while APU is off)
- Sample pipeline: fractional M-cycle accumulator at 44,100 Hz default
- DAC on/off per channel; read-only/write-only bit masking per spec

Wire into platform:
- DmgBus: owns Apu, routes $FF10–$FF3F reads/writes, ticks APU each
  M-cycle, exposes sample_ready()/take_sample()/set_audio_sample_rate()
- GameBoy: forwards sample_ready()/get_sample()/set_audio_sample_rate()
- Console::GameBoy in emulator.rs: dispatches all three audio methods

Tests: 76 new unit tests (15 APU skeleton, 22 CH1, 10 CH2, 15 CH3,
14 CH4, 7 bus integration, 5 GameBoy console) — 421 GB tests total.

Update architecture.md with new APU module descriptions.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* fix(gb): drain APU samples in native event loop; return $FF on powered-off reads

Address PR #2028 review comments:

- Wire up GB audio in native frontend: Console::GameBoy arm in event_loop.rs
  now drains sample_ready()/get_sample() into the audio device each tick,
  matching the NES sample-drain pattern. This was the root cause of silence
  when running GB ROMs.

- Implement powered-off APU reads: Apu::read_register now returns $FF for
  NR10-NR51 ($FF10-$FF25) when the APU is powered off, matching the doc
  comment and SameBoy/DMG behavior. NR52 and wave RAM remain accessible.

- Fix misleading test: test_apu_registers_return_ff_when_powered_off now
  asserts $FF (not $00) for NR50/NR51 reads when APU is powered off, making
  the test name, comment, and assertion internally consistent.

Add C++ RSP SSH ProxyCommand client (rsp_ssh)

rsp_ssh.cpp connects to an RSP resource manager using protobuf encoding,
opens a socket to an sshd exposed via RSP using connectTCPSocket(), then
bridges stdin/stdout to that socket via two pump threads so that the ssh
client communicates transparently.

Usage:
  ssh -o ProxyCommand='rsp_ssh /etc/rsp-ssh/rsp_ssh.conf.json' user@host

Or via ~/.ssh/config ProxyCommand directive.

- integration/openssh/rsp_ssh.cpp: C++ source
- Makefile: RSP_SSH_TARGET + RSP_SSH_OBJECTS, added to `all` and rsp-ssh phony target
- example/rsp_ssh.conf.json: config template
- modification/README.md: client usage docs

rpc: implement registercsapp, stopcsapp, updatecsapp commands

Add the three write RPC commands for the CSApp subsystem:

- registercsapp <deployment_id> <spec_json> <owner> <locked_cscoin> [ip_hint]
  Builds a CSAPP_REGISTER_TX_VERSION transaction, signs the data with the
  owner's wallet key via CObfuScationSigner, and commits via CommitTransaction.

- stopcsapp <deployment_id> <owner>
  Builds a CSAPP_STOP_TX_TYPE transaction and commits it.

- updatecsapp <deployment_id> <spec_json> <owner>
  Builds a CSAPP_UPDATE_TX_TYPE transaction and commits it.

All three commands follow the same pattern as fluxnode RPCs:
CMutableTransaction → sign with CObfuScationSigner → CWalletTx → CommitTransaction.

Signing uses DecodeDestination + pwalletMain->GetKey to retrieve the owner's
private key; the signature message covers all relevant tx fields so nodes can
verify authenticity on chain.
